raw.title: Reinforced Masonry Wall — ASD
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse Reinforced Masonry Wall — ASD 🖨 Print / PDF ⇓ Save ⇑ Load Reinforced Masonry Wall & Parapet — ASD 8″, 10″, 12″ CMU · Single / Double Reinforced · Partial / Full Grout · TMS 402-22 / MDG-2022 TMS 402-22 MDG-2022 Ch. 11 §8.3.4 Axial & Flexure §8.3.5 Shear §8.4 Deflection ASCE/SEI 7-22 🧱 Wall (Pin–Pin) 🏗 Parapet (Cantilever) → Import from other ARE calculators: 📺 Open C&C Wind Calc ▶ Load C&C from Memory 📺 Open Diaphragm Calc ▶ Load Diaphragm from Memory ASD Method (TMS 402-22 Ch. 8): This calculator uses Allowable Stress Design. All loads are entered at service level (unfactored) — the calculator does not apply load factors internally. For strength design (LRFD slender wall per Ch. 9), use Enercalc or a comparable program. ASD and LRFD will give different numerical results; both are valid per TMS 402-22. WALL SECTION — OUT-OF-PLANE LOADING As (tension) — grouted cell w = 20.0 psf P h = 12.0 ft t = 7.63″ d = 3.81″ EXT. INT. Wall Section — Out-of-Plane CMU PLAN VIEW (LOOKING DOWN) grout grout As EXTERIOR FACE INTERIOR FACE t = 7.63″ s = 32″ o.c. | 7 cells @ 8″ = 56″ CMU Plan View (Looking Down) IN-PLANE SHEAR — WALL ELEVATION V = 500 plf R Lw = 20.0 ft h = 12.0 ft Vert. @ 32″ Horiz. @ 48″ In-Plane Shear — Wall Elevation ● Wall Configuration CMU Size & Reinforcement Nominal Wall Size 8″ CMU (t = 7.625″) 10″ CMU (t = 9.625″) 12″ CMU (t = 11.625″) Grout Condition Partially Grouted (rebar cells only) Fully Grouted Reinforcement Type Single Reinforced (tension side only) Double Reinforced (tension + compression) ℹ 8″ CMU: t = 7.625″, tfs = 1.25″ | An (partial) = 30.0 in²/ft | An (full) = 91.5 in²/ft | Suggested d (mid-depth) = 3.81″ ● Material Properties TMS 402-22 §4.2 Masonry Type CMU (concrete masonry) Clay brick masonry f′m — Specified Compressive Strength psi 1,500 psi 2,000 psi 2,500 psi 3,000 psi 3,500 psi Steel Grade Grade 60 (Fs = 32,000 psi) Grade 40/50 (Fs = 20,000 psi) ℹ Em = 900 × 2000 = 1800000 psi | n = 16.11 | Fb = 0.45 × 2000 = 900 psi | Fs = 32000 psi ● Wall Geometry, Axial Loads & Reinforcement IBC ASD Combos Design Span h ft Unsupported height for slenderness & OOP moment Wall Self-Weight — Dead Load, e = 0 (centered on wall): Wall Weight psf Weight per sf of wall face area Total Wall Height ft Full height above design point (can exceed design span for parapet, upper floors) Pwall plf 660 = Weight × Height | Applied at wall centroid, no eccentricity Applied Axial Loads — with eccentricity (from bearing, ledger, joist seat, etc.): Dead PD plf Live PL plf Roof Live PLr plf Snow PS plf Wind PW plf Vertical component (+dn / −uplift) Eccentricity e in From wall CL to load line of action ▶ Governing IBC ASD Combo: D + 0.6W | Ptotal = 660 plf | includes OOP wind | Unity ≈ 0.095 Vertical (Out-of-Plane) Reinforcement — Tension Side: OOP Bar Size #4 (0.20 in²) #5 (0.31 in²) #6 (0.44 in²) #7 (0.60 in²) #8 (0.79 in²) #9 (1.00 in²) OOP Bar Spacing s in 8″ 16″ 24″ 32″ 40″ 48″ 56″ C-C spacing of vertical bars (8″ cell grid) Effective Depth d in From comp. face to tension steel CL. Suggested: 3.81″ (mid-depth). Compression Steel (Double Reinforced) — Compression Side: Comp. Bar Size #4 (0.20 in²) #5 (0.31 in²) #6 (0.44 in²) #7 (0.60 in²) #8 (0.79 in²) Comp. Bar Spacing in Comp. Steel Depth d′ in From compression face to compression steel CL ● Out-of-Plane Loading OOP Wind / Seismic — Service Level Service Wind Pressure w psf (unfactored — ASD) Net OOP design pressure at service level. For C&C wind: use the direct zone pressure (do not multiply by load factors). Import from C&C Calc above. OOP Moment Calculation Simply Supported: M = wh²/8 Cantilever (fixed base): M = wh²/2 Fixed-Fixed (top & base): M = wh²/12 Manual Moment Input Manual Moop lb·ft/ft Moment per unit width of wall (per ft), service level ● In-Plane Shear Shear Wall Demand — Service Level Unit Shear v plf V/Lw in plf. Import from Diaphragm Calc above. Wall Length Lw ft Length of the shear wall segment M / (V · dv) Ratio Clamped to [0.25, 1.0] per TMS 402-22 §8.3.5 Horizontal (In-Plane Shear) Reinforcement: Horiz. Bar Size None (Fvs = 0) #4 (0.20 in²) #5 (0.31 in²) #6 (0.44 in²) Horiz. Bar Spacing s in Vertical spacing between horizontal bars ▶ Run All Checks ✔ PASS — Max D/C = 0.332 8″ CMU | partial grout | Single Reinforced | f′m = 2000 psi | Grade 60 | h = 12.0ft | w = 20.0 psf (service) ⇄ ResultsGov. axial demand: 660 lb/ft→Total OOP moment: 360 lb-ft/ftIn-plane shear (full wall): 10 kips→Allowable axial Pa: 18,319 lb/ftOOP deflection: 0.02 inMax D/C ratio: 0.33 Section & Material Properties Property Value Wall size / actual thickness 8″ nominal, t = 7.63″ Face shell tfs 1.25″ Grout Partially Grouted An (net area) 30.0 in²/ft In (net inertia) 308.7 in&sup4;/ft r (radius of gyration) 3.208″ h/r slenderness 44.89 ≤ 99 (Eq. 8-16) Em 1800000 psi n = Es/Em 16.11 Fb = 0.45f′m 900.0 psi Fs 32000 psi γg (shear grout factor) 0.7 As vertical #5 @ 32.0″ = 0.116 in²/ft, d = 3.81″ Av horizontal #4 @ 48.0″ = 0.050 in²/ft Applied Demands (Service Level) Demand Value Gov. IBC ASD Combo D + 0.6W (w/ wind) Pwall (self-weight) 660 plf (55 psf × 12.0 ft, e = 0) Ptotal (governing) 660 lb/ft OOP wind w 20.0 psf (service, unfactored) Mwind 360.00 lb·ft/ft Mecc = Papp×e 0.00 lb·ft/ft Mtotal OOP 360.00 lb·ft/ft Voop 120.0 lb/ft In-plane unit shear v 500 plf In-plane total V 10.00 kips (Lw = 20.0ft) Deflection δ / allowed 0.017″ / 0.960″ 660 lb/ft Axial Ptotal D + 0.6W 360.00 lb·ft/ft Total OOP Moment 217.9 psi / 900.0 psi fb / Fb (masonry) 10633.3 psi / 32000 psi fs / Fs (steel) 500 plf In-Plane Unit Shear 16.67 psi / 54.00 psi fv / Fv (in-plane) 0.017″ OOP Deflection 0.332 Max D/C Ratio Code Checks — ASD (TMS 402-22) Check Reference Demand Allowable D/C Status Detail Axial Compression h/r = 44.89 | R = 0.897 | D + 0.6W TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2 P = 660 lb/ft Pa = 18319 lb/ft 0.036 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.2 — Allowable Axial Compression (Reinforced) Eq. 8-16: P a = (0.30f′ m A n + 0.65A st F s ) × R where R = 1 − (h/140r) 2 h/r = 144.00″ / 3.21″ = 44.89 ≤ 99 → Eq. 8-16 R = 1 − (44.89/140) 2 = 0.8972 P a = (0.30 × 2000 × 30.0 + 0.65 × 0.116 × 32000) × 0.8972 = (18000 + 2418) × 0.8972 = 18319 lb/ft f a = P/A n = 660/30.0 = 22.00 psi D/C = P/P a = 660 18319 = 0.036 OOP Flexure — Masonry k = 0.248 | j = 0.9173 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fb = 217.9 psi Fb = 900.0 psi 0.242 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 / MDG-2022 §11.4.1 — OOP Flexure: Masonry Compression (fb) Inputs: b = 12″, d = 3.81″, A s = 0.116 in²/ft, n = 16.11 Gov. Combo: D + 0.6W Mwind: 360.00 lb·ft/ft Mtotal: 360.00 lb·ft/ft → 4320 lb·in/ft Eq. 11.4-1: ρ = 0.116/(12×3.81) = 0.0025 nρ = 16.11 × 0.0025 = 0.0410 Eq. 11.4-2: k = √ (nρ)2+2nρ − nρ = 0.2482 j = 1 − k/3 = 0.9173 Eq. 11.4-3: f b = 2M jkbd2 = 2 × 4320 0.9173×0.2482×12×3.812 = 217.9 psi F b = 0.45f′ m = 0.45 × 2000 = 900.0 psi D/C masonry = 217.9 900.0 = 0.242 OOP Flexure — Steel As = 0.116 in²/ft | ρ = 0.0025 TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 fs = 10633.3 psi Fs = 32000 psi 0.332 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.2 / MDG-2022 §11.4.1 — OOP Flexure: Steel Tension (fs) k = 0.2482 j = 0.9173 d = 3.81″ A s = 0.116 in²/ft Eq. 11.4-4: f s = M As·j·d = 4320 0.116×0.9173×3.81 = 10633.3 psi F s = 32000 psi (Grade 60) D/C steel = 10633.3 32000 = 0.332 Combined Axial + Flexure fa/Fa = 0.036 | fb/Fb = 0.242 TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.1 fa/Fa + fb/Fb ≤ 1.0 0.278 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.4.1 — Combined Axial Compression + Flexure Gov. IBC ASD Combo: D + 0.6W (includes OOP wind) Ptotal: P wall (660 × 1) + applied = 660 lb/ft Eq. 8-14: fa Fa + fb Fb ≤ 1.0 f a /F a = 22.00/610.63 = 0.036 f b /F b = 217.9/900.0 = 0.242 Unity = 0.036 + 0.242 = 0.278 OOP Shear V = 120.0 lb/ft | γg = 0.7 TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.2 fv = 4.00 psi Fv = 39.07 psi 0.102 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.2 — Out-of-Plane Shear Voop: wh/2 = 20.0×12.0/2 = 120.0 lb/ft fv: 120.0/30.0 = 4.00 psi M/Vdv for OOP » 1.0 (governs upper limit) Fvm: 0.5(4−1.75)√f′ m + 0.25f a = 55.81 psi Fv,oop: min(55.81, 2√2000) × 0.7 = 39.07 psi D/C = 4.00 39.07 = 0.102 In-Plane Shear Fvm=55.81 | Fvs=21.33 psi TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.1 fv = 16.67 psi Fv = 54.00 psi 0.309 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.3.5.1.1 / MDG-2022 §11.4.3 — In-Plane Shear fv: v/A n = 500/30.0 = 16.67 psi M/Vdv: 1.000 (clamped [0.25, 1.0]) Fvm: 0.5(4−1.75×1.000)√2000 + 0.25f a = 55.81 psi Fvs: 0.5×(A v /s)×F s ×9.6/A n = 0.5×0.0042×32000×9.6/30.0 = 21.33 psi Fv,max: 2.0√f′ m = 89.44 psi Fv,ip: min(89.44, 77.14) × γ g (0.7) = 54.00 psi D/C = 16.67 54.00 = 0.309 OOP Deflection Ieff=308.7 in&sup4;/ft | Icr=18.7 in&sup4;/ft TMS 402-22 §8.4 δ = 0.017″ h/150 = 0.960″ 0.017 PASS ► Calc TMS 402-22 §8.4 — OOP Deflection (ASD, Branson's Ieff) fr: 2.5√f′ m = 111.8 psi (TMS Table 11.3.1) Sn: I n /(t/2) = 308.7/3.813 = 80.97 in³/ft Mcr: f r ×S n = 111.8×80.97 = 9053 lb·in/ft = 754.42 lb·ft/ft Mtotal: 4320 lb·in/ft Section: UNCRACKED (M ≤ M cr ) → I eff = I n = 308.7 in&sup4;/ft δ: (5/384) × (w/12) × h 4 / (E m ×I eff ) = 0.01302 × (20.0/12) × 144.0 4 / (1800000×308.7) = 0.017″ δallow: h/150 = 144.0/150 = 0.960″ D/C = 0.017 0.960 = 0.017 Engineering Notes: ASD vs. Strength Design: This calculator uses ASD (TMS 402-22 Ch. 8). Enercalc's "Masonry Slender Wall" uses Strength Design (Ch. 9) with factored loads and φ factors — different method, not a bug. For the same wall geometry, ASD and LRFD typically give different numerical results; verify the appropriate method with the project EOR. All service loads are entered unfactored. The calculator applies no load factors internally. Deflection uses Branson's effective moment of inertia (Ieff). The TMS Ch. 9 Slender Wall method uses Icr directly, yielding larger deflections. Verify δallow per ASCE 7 or project specs (h/150 used here). Axial allowable Pa includes steel contribution (0.65AstFs) per Eq. 8-16/17. Eccentricity e adds moment Me = P×e/12 to wind moment for combined OOP demand. In-plane Fvs derived per-foot using dv = 0.8×12 = 9.6 in (unit-width basis). For full-wall check verify dv = 0.8Lw. Partial grout γg = 0.70 reduces allowable shear per TMS 402-22 §8.3.5. → Import parapet wind pressure from C&C Calc: ▶ Load Parapet Pressure from Memory Parapet — ASD (TMS 402-22 Ch. 8): Parapet modeled as a cantilever fixed at roof bond beam. M = w×hp²/2 | V = w×hp. Effective height for Fa: heff = 2hp (fixed–free). Service-level loads. Ref: TMS 402-22 §8.3 / MDG-2022 Ch. 11. PARAPET SECTION — CANTILEVER As free w (psf) hp t M₀ base Parapet Section WALL + PARAPET ELEVATION roof WALL PARAPET h hp Wall + Parapet Elevation FREE BODY AT FIXED BASE V = w·hp M = w·hp²/2 V = w × hp M = w × hp² / 2 Free Body at Fixed Base 1. audit.dimensions: PASS: h=12 ft, t=7.63, d=3.81 and plan spacing labels match fixture/math
missing_pre_fix: out-of-plane wall section is an anonymous strip without block geometry
audit.note: post-fix re-judgment 2026-07-11 (Claude reviewer): all required elements present in rebuilt diagram; screenshots in tools/masonry-audit/diagram-review/. Pre-fix failure retained in git history (commit 9c3596f).
